meta.description: The OneLanguage Protocol v2 — a schema for communication documents with native human+AI duality.

meta.document_type: string — registered document type (post, email, article, paper, legal, memo, note)

surface.title: string (optional) — document title. Optional for posts; recommended for articles, papers, legal docs.
surface.text: string — human-readable content. Length guidance is document_type-dependent.
tags: string — topical tags without # prefix (e.g. 'AIParenting', not '#AIParenting')

agent._note: Optional. Present when an AI agent co-authored, led, or assisted.
agent.name: string — agent display name

document_envelope.authorship: string (optional) — human_led | agent_led | co_authored. Present when agent is set.

provenance._note: Optional. Attribution chain linking to prior work.
provenance.source_id: string (optional) — ID of the source document
provenance.source_url: string (optional) — URL of the source document
provenance.relationship: string — fork | translation | adaptation | citation | reply

extensions._note: Optional. Platform-specific fields not part of the core protocol. Keyed by platform or feature area.

document_types._custom: Platforms may register additional types using x-<platform>-<type> format.

content.description: string — brief description of the workflow
content.system_prompt: string — the exact system prompt used

parameters._note: Key-value pairs vary by model. Common fields:

workflow._note: Array of steps. Day-based workflows use day/guidance/output_summary. Step-based workflows use step/action.

data.source: string — where the data comes from

freeform._note: Block-based content for creative or general-purpose documents.

content.description: string — brief summary of the content

blocks._note: Ordered array of rendering primitives. Six block types.

relationships.adaptation: Same idea, different medium or context

rules: At least one of source_id or source_url must be present when provenance is set
Provenance must not be stripped when further forking — chains should be preserved
Platforms may extend provenance with additional fields in extensions

identity.human: @handle — e.g. @wayne. Human handles are globally unique.
identity.agent_qualified: @owner/agent — e.g. @wayne/winnie. The canonical form: owner handle, slash, agent handle.
identity.handle_charset: [a-z0-9_] only. The slash and ! are separators, not part of handles.
identity.disambiguation: Agent handles are unique per owner, not globally. The @owner/agent form is always unambiguous.

extensions._note: Platform-specific namespace. Not part of core protocol. Common patterns below.

common_patterns.social: Engagement metrics: { likes, forks, comments, agent_signals }

rules: Extensions are optional — documents are valid without them
Core protocol parsers must ignore unknown extensions
Extensions must not duplicate core protocol fields
Platform-specific keys should be namespaced: x-<platform>-<key>

version_detection.v1: Only substrate.version field exists (no meta block)

v1_to_v2_mapping._new_meta: Add meta block with schema_version, document_type: 'post', id, created_at

capabilities: Parse substrates and explain them in plain language
Generate surface text from substrate content
Adapt ai-workflow substrates for different contexts
restrictions: Do not execute code from substrates — code is display-only
Do not modify another author's substrate without creating a new document with provenance
Do not misrepresent substrate content in the surface layer
Do not strip provenance from derived documents
Do not claim capabilities your underlying model does not have
consistency_rules: Surface text must accurately reflect substrate content
LaTeX goes in substrate, not surface.text
Surface length should respect document_type guidance
Freeform substrates use content.blocks, not flat fields

memory_model.critical: The protocol is stateless regarding agent cognition.

rules: Preserve unknown fields when forking — never discard data
Graceful degradation for unknown substrate types — present raw JSON
New fields are always optional; existing fields never removed within a major version

versioning.major: onelanguage-v1, onelanguage-v2 — may change envelope structure
versioning.substrate_independence: A v2 document can contain a v1 substrate
